title: ABC Initiative Implementation Grants 2026: Up to $150,000 in Funding for Nonprofit VITA Organizations Supporting Child Savings Access
summary: Access to savings opportunities from an early age can play a transformative role in helping children build long-term financial security. Recognizing this, Prosperity Now , with support from the Citi Foundation , has launched the ABC Initiative (Asset Building for Children) and is now accepting applications for its first round of Implementation Grants . Through this initiative, eligible nonprofit organizations operating IRS-sponsored Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) programs can receive grants ranging from $25,000 to $150,000 to help families access child savings opportunities during tax season. Backed by $5 million in funding from Citi Foundation , the ABC Initiative seeks to strengthen financial inclusion by integrating child savings education, referrals, and support into tax-time services. The initiative focuses on helping families understand emerging child savings opportunities, including Section 530A Accounts , while equipping nonprofit organizations with the tools, training, and financial resources needed to expand their impact. If your nonprofit organization operates a VITA program and serves low- and moderate-income families, this grant opportunity provides significant funding and technical support to strengthen your services while contributing to a national effort to improve children s financial futures. About the ABC Initiative The Asset Building for Children (ABC) Initiative is a national program led by Prosperity Now to help more families access child savings opportunities across the United States. The initiative was created to ensure that families receive clear information, practical guidance, and personalized support in understanding and accessing child savings accounts. Through this effort, Prosperity Now aims to increase participation in existing and emerging child savings programs while reducing barriers that prevent many families from taking advantage of these opportunities. Supported by a $5 million investment from the Citi Foundation , the first phase of the initiative focuses on helping nonprofit organizations integrate child savings guidance into IRS-sponsored Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) services. Why the ABC Initiative Matters Tax season is often one of the most important financial periods for many families. During this time, households make key decisions regarding: Tax refunds Tax credits Household budgets Savings Future financial planning Because VITA programs already provide trusted tax preparation services to millions of families, Prosperity Now believes these organizations are ideally positioned to educate parents about child savings opportunities while helping them make informed financial decisions. The initiative seeks to ensure that more children begin adulthood with stronger financial foundations by connecting families with savings opportunities during one of their most financially significant moments of the year. Grant Funding Available Eligible organizations selected for the first implementation cohort will receive grants ranging from: Minimum Award: $25,000 Maximum Award: $150,000 Final grant amounts will depend on several factors, including: Anticipated number of families served. Scope of the proposed implementation. Organizational capacity. Proposed project activities. Budget quality. Overall funding availability. What the Grant Will Support Funding can be used to strengthen tax-time services by incorporating child savings education and support into existing VITA operations. Examples of eligible activities include: Integrating child savings information into VITA intake and tax preparation workflows. Providing child savings education during tax preparation appointments. Offering referrals and navigation support for child savings opportunities. Conducting community outreach before and during tax season. Training staff and volunteers on child savings programs. Developing partnerships with financial institutions and community organizations. Hosting tax-season outreach and activation events. Improving internal systems and workflows. Collecting implementation data and participating in evaluation activities. Supporting continuous improvement of service delivery. Additional Support Beyond Grant Funding Successful applicants will receive much more than financial support. Awardees will benefit from: Comprehensive onboarding. Professional training. Ongoing technical assistance. Implementation guidance. Digital financial coaching resources where applicable. Practical implementation tools. Peer learning opportunities. Participation in a national learning community. Evaluation support. Standardized reporting guidance. Opportunities to contribute to national best practices. Who Can Apply? Applications are invited from eligible nonprofit organizations that operate IRS-sponsored Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) programs. To qualify, organizations must: Be a nonprofit organization. Operate an IRS-sponsored VITA program. Serve as the lead applicant and fiscal agent. Be responsible for grant management and reporting. Demonstrate readiness to integrate child savings support into tax-time services. Serve low- and moderate-income households. Have strong community relationships. Participate fully in training, evaluation, and peer learning. Community organizations, schools, financial institutions, and public agencies may participate as partners, but the lead applicant must be an eligible VITA provider. Geographic Eligibility Priority will be given to organizations serving the following locations: California Alameda Contra Costa Fresno Kern Kings Los Angeles Marin Merced Orange Riverside Sacramento San Bernardino San Diego San Francisco San Joaquin San Mateo Santa Clara Stanislaus Ventura Connecticut Greater Bridgeport Planning Region Western Connecticut Planning Region Florida Broward Miami-Dade Palm Beach Illinois Cook DuPage Kane Lake McHenry Will Maryland Montgomery Prince George s Nevada Clark County New Jersey Bergen Essex Hudson Passaic New York Bronx Kings Nassau New York Queens Richmond Rockland Suffolk Westchester Puerto Rico Territory-wide South Dakota State-wide Virginia Alexandria City Arlington Fairfax Fairfax City Falls Church City Washington, D.C. Entire District of Columbia APPLY HERE Preferred Applicant Experience Organizations are encouraged to have experience in areas such as: Children s Savings Accounts Section 530A Accounts Baby Bonds 529 Savings Plans Financial coaching Tax-time savings initiatives Family financial capability However, prior child savings experience is not required . Organizations operating only Tax Counseling for the Elderly (TCE) programs are not eligible . Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Quality of the implementation approach. Organizational capacity and readiness. Family and community reach. Strength of partnerships. Evaluation and learning capacity. Budget quality and feasibility. Applicants should clearly demonstrate how they will integrate child savings education into their existing VITA services. Participation Requirements Successful organizations will be expected to: Implement the approved project. Attend onboarding sessions. Participate in required training. Join technical assistance activities. Engage in peer learning. Train staff and volunteers. Build strategic partnerships. Collect and report project data. Participate in programme evaluation. Share lessons learned with the national cohort. Reporting Requirements Awardees will submit both quantitative and qualitative reports covering: Staff and volunteer training completion. Families reached. Child savings interactions. Referrals made. Account enrollments where applicable. Partnerships established. Organizational capacity improvements. Lessons learned. Implementation challenges. Best practices. Reporting guidance and standardized tools will be provided during onboarding. Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the following timeline: Activity Date Request for Proposals Released July 20, 2026 Application Deadline August 7, 2026 Conditional Award Notifications September 2, 2026 Final Award Agreements Public Announcement September 16, 2026 ABC Initiative Cohort Launch VITACon October 6, 2026 Training Preparation Fall 2026 2027 Tax Season Implementation January–April 2027 How to Apply Interested organizations should: Review all eligibility requirements. Download and study the 2026 Request for Proposals (RFP). Review the Application Planning Guide. Gather all required documentation. Submit their application through Prosperity Now s online application portal no later than August 7, 2026 . Applicants with questions may contact Prosperity Now through the email address provided in the official call for proposals. Why Organizations Should Apply Participating organizations will benefit from: Grant funding of up to $150,000. National recognition through participation in the ABC Initiative. Capacity-building support. Staff and volunteer training. Technical assistance. Access to implementation resources. Peer networking opportunities. Evaluation support. Opportunities to strengthen services for low-income families. The ability to contribute to improving financial security for children across the United States. Visit HERE to learn more about the ABC Initiative Implementation Grants 2026 Deadline Application Deadline: August 7, 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered. Eligible organizations are encouraged to begin preparing their applications as early as possible. Conclusion The ABC Initiative Implementation Grants 2026 represent an outstanding funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations committed to expanding financial opportunity for children and families. Through generous grants of up to $150,000 , extensive technical assistance, professional training, and participation in a national learning community, Prosperity Now is equipping VITA providers to become trusted pathways for child savings education and access. Organizations serving low- and moderate-income families have a unique opportunity to strengthen their tax-time services while helping families build long-term financial security for future generations. If your organization operates an eligible IRS-sponsored VITA program and serves one of the target communities, this initiative offers both the financial resources and strategic support needed to make a lasting impact. For more grant opportunities visit OFY HERE The post ABC Initiative Implementation Grants 2026: Up to $150,000 in Funding for Nonprofit VITA Organizations Supporting Child Savings Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Experienced Consultant Positions with Boston Consulting Group (BCG) in the USA 2026: Apply for High-Paying Consulting Careers with Salaries Starting at $190,000
summary: Are you an experienced professional looking to take your consulting career to the next level with one of the world s most prestigious management consulting firms? Boston Consulting Group (BCG) is currently inviting applications for Experienced Consultant Positions across multiple locations in the United States. This is an exceptional opportunity for talented professionals who are passionate about solving complex business challenges, driving innovation, and creating meaningful impact for leading organizations worldwide. Founded in 1963, Boston Consulting Group (BCG) has built a global reputation as one of the pioneers of modern business strategy. Today, BCG continues to help businesses, governments, and organizations navigate transformation by combining deep industry expertise, cutting-edge technology, and innovative thinking. Through its collaborative work environment and world-class professional development programs, BCG offers consultants the opportunity to work alongside some of the brightest minds in the consulting industry while accelerating their own careers. If you have consulting experience, an advanced degree, or a strong desire to solve real-world business problems, this could be the perfect opportunity to join a globally recognized organization that values diversity, collaboration, and continuous learning. About Boston Consulting Group (BCG) Boston Consulting Group (BCG) is one of the world s leading management consulting firms, partnering with businesses, governments, non-profit organizations, and institutions to solve their most critical challenges. Since its establishment in 1963 , BCG has been at the forefront of business strategy, helping organizations: Develop competitive advantages. Lead digital transformation initiatives. Improve organizational performance. Build sustainable growth strategies. Navigate complex business environments. Deliver measurable financial impact. Today, BCG combines traditional management consulting with expertise in: Digital transformation Artificial Intelligence Corporate strategy Technology consulting Innovation Sustainability Business design Organizational transformation Corporate ventures Its collaborative consulting model enables teams to work closely with clients at every organizational level, delivering practical solutions that create long-term value. About the Experienced Consultant Opportunity BCG is recruiting Experienced Hires for both Associate and Consultant positions across 11 locations in the United States . Successful candidates will become part of multidisciplinary consulting teams working on some of the world s most challenging business issues. As a consultant, you will collaborate with industry experts, business leaders, government agencies, and international organizations to develop innovative strategies that transform organizations and improve business performance. Every project provides an opportunity to expand your professional knowledge while building valuable leadership and consulting skills. What You Will Do As an Experienced Consultant at BCG, you will work on high-impact projects that address strategic challenges across various industries. Your responsibilities may include: Collaborating with experienced consultants and subject matter experts. Analyzing complex business problems. Developing innovative business strategies. Conducting research and data analysis. Presenting recommendations to clients. Supporting organizational transformation projects. Delivering measurable business results. Building long-term client relationships. Working directly with senior executives. Contributing to multidisciplinary consulting teams. Because every client engagement is unique, consultants gain exposure to numerous industries and business environments throughout their careers. Types of Projects You May Work On Consulting engagements at BCG are highly diverse and may include projects such as: Designing integration strategies following major pharmaceutical mergers. Developing sustainable agricultural practices in emerging economies. Supporting digital transformation initiatives. Improving operational efficiency. Creating growth strategies. Developing AI-driven business solutions. Organizational restructuring. Customer experience transformation. Supply chain optimization. Public sector strategy development. Projects vary in: Duration Team size Industry sector Client location Complexity Many projects involve working directly at client sites, and international travel may be required depending on business needs. Career Development at BCG One of the key advantages of joining Boston Consulting Group is its strong commitment to professional growth. Employees benefit from: Dedicated mentorship. Personalized career development. World-class leadership training. Continuous learning opportunities. Exposure to global clients. Cross-functional project experience. Opportunities to specialize in industry practice areas. Access to expert consulting tracks. As consultants gain experience, they may choose to specialize in particular industries or functional disciplines, becoming recognized experts within the firm. Available Positions BCG is hiring experienced professionals for the following primary roles: Associate Suitable for candidates with: 0–4 years of full-time work experience. Consultant Suitable for candidates with: 4 or more years of professional experience. Or an advanced degree such as: MBA PhD Equivalent postgraduate qualifications Applicants should select the position that best matches their qualifications. If there is uncertainty regarding the appropriate level, BCG will assess candidates during the recruitment process and determine the most suitable role. Additional Career Opportunities BCG is also recruiting for several specialized positions, including: Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, Organization Design Operating Model Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, B2B SaaS (Client Focus Track) Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, B2B SaaS (Topic Activation Track) Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, Indirect Procurement in Retail Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, Automotive Mobility Manager – BCG Vantage, Consumer People and Organization Manager – BCG Vantage, Financial Institutions – Wealth Management BCG Platinion – Senior AI Tech Architect BCG Platinion – Senior AI Tech Architect (AI Platforms) These opportunities are available across multiple U.S. locations and require varying levels of consulting or industry experience. Eligibility Requirements To apply, candidates should meet the following criteria: Apply for the role that best matches your professional experience. Complete all degree programs before your employment start date. Possess the required work experience for the chosen position. Hold U.S. work authorization. Meet any specific educational or professional requirements outlined for specialized positions. Work Locations Successful applicants may be based in any of the following U.S. offices: Boston, Massachusetts Atlanta, Georgia Chicago, Illinois Summit, New Jersey Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Seattle, Washington San Francisco, California Denver, Colorado Los Angeles, California San Diego, California Washington, D.C. Depending on project assignments, consultants may also travel internationally. Salary and Benefits BCG offers one of the most competitive compensation packages in the consulting industry. Successful candidates will receive: Base Salary Starting base compensation of $190,000 per year . Additional Financial Benefits Employees are also eligible for: Annual discretionary performance bonus. Profit Sharing and Retirement Fund (PSRF) contributions. Employee Benefits BCG provides a comprehensive benefits package, including: Competitive retirement benefits. Professional development opportunities. Mentorship programmes. Leadership training. Career coaching. Learning and development resources. Access to a collaborative global consulting network. Diversity and Equal Employment Opportunity Boston Consulting Group is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where every employee has the opportunity to succeed. Employment decisions are made without discrimination based on: Race National origin Religion Age Colour Sex Sexual orientation Gender identity Disability Protected veteran status Any other status protected under applicable federal, state, or local laws BCG actively seeks to build diverse teams that reflect a broad range of experiences and perspectives. Why You Should Apply Joining Boston Consulting Group offers numerous advantages, including: Opportunity to work with one of the world s leading consulting firms. Highly competitive salary. Annual bonuses. Retirement contributions. Professional mentorship. Exposure to global clients. International travel opportunities. Career advancement pathways. Continuous professional development. Collaborative work environment. Diverse and inclusive workplace. Opportunity to solve high-impact business challenges. APPLY HERE Application Process Interested candidates should: Review the available Associate or Consultant position. Confirm that they meet the eligibility requirements. Ensure they possess U.S. work authorization. Prepare their application materials. Submit an online application through the official Boston Consulting Group careers portal. Applicants interested in specialized roles should review the specific requirements before applying. Deadline Application Deadline: Rolling until Filled Since applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of being considered. Visit HERE to learn more about the Experienced Consultant Positions with Boston Consulting Group (BCG) in the USA 2026 Conclusion The Boston Consulting Group Experienced Consultant Positions 2026 present an outstanding opportunity for experienced professionals seeking to join one of the world s most respected consulting firms. With a starting salary of $190,000 , outstanding benefits, global exposure, structured mentorship, and opportunities to work on transformational projects across industries, BCG provides an exceptional environment for career growth. Whether you are an experienced consultant, an MBA graduate, a PhD holder, or an accomplished professional looking to transition into management consulting, BCG offers the resources, network, and career development opportunities needed to excel. If you meet the eligibility requirements and have U.S. work authorization, consider applying early, as recruitment will continue on a rolling basis until all positions have been filled. For more job opportunities visit OFY HERE The post Experienced Consultant Positions with Boston Consulting Group (BCG) in the USA 2026: Apply for High-Paying Consulting Careers with Salaries Starting at $190,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mid-Career Course FY2026 in Japan: Fully Funded Leadership and Career Development Program for Peacebuilding and Development Professionals at Hiroshima University
summary: Professionals working in peacebuilding, humanitarian response, international development, governance, and related sectors now have an outstanding opportunity to strengthen their leadership skills and expand their global professional network through the Mid-Career Course FY2026 , organized by Hiroshima University under the Program for Global Human Resource Development for Peacebuilding and Development (GPAD) . Scheduled to take place from January 7 to January 13, 2027 , in Hiroshima and Tokyo, Japan , this prestigious international training program is supported by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan (MOFA) and implemented by Hiroshima University in cooperation with the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) . Designed specifically for experienced mid-career professionals, the course focuses on leadership development, career advancement, resilience, and international networking while providing participants with the opportunity to learn from globally recognized experts and visit the historic city of Hiroshima to understand its remarkable journey of peace, recovery, and transformation. Whether you are currently working with international organizations, governments, NGOs, humanitarian agencies, or the private sector, this program provides an exceptional platform to sharpen your leadership abilities and prepare for senior roles in peacebuilding and sustainable development. About the Mid-Career Course FY2026 The Mid-Career Course is one of the flagship initiatives under the Global Peace and Development Career Network (GPAD) . The course aims to empower experienced professionals by helping them: Develop stronger leadership capabilities. Reflect on their professional journey. Build confidence in managing teams. Improve career planning. Strengthen professional identity. Expand international professional networks. Learn practical leadership approaches from experienced practitioners. Gain inspiration from Hiroshima s transformation into a global symbol of peace. Participants, referred to as Program Associates (PAs) , will participate in intensive workshops, collaborative discussions, leadership exercises, networking events, and field learning experiences. The program combines classroom learning, self-reflection, peer learning, coaching, and experiential learning. Program Objectives By the end of the training, participants will have developed practical leadership and management competencies that can immediately be applied in their workplaces. Participants will be able to: Assess their own leadership strengths and weaknesses. Identify their preferred leadership style. Understand how leadership approaches influence team performance. Develop strategies for becoming more effective managers. Strengthen decision-making skills. Improve communication and team-building capabilities. Reflect on their career achievements and future aspirations. Build a compelling professional narrative. Understand resilience through Hiroshima s history and reconstruction. Expand international professional relationships. What Participants Will Learn The course offers comprehensive professional development in several key areas. 1. Leadership Development Participants will explore: Leadership styles Adaptive leadership Team management Organizational leadership Decision-making Conflict management Communication strategies Leadership values Ethical leadership 2. Career Development The course helps professionals: Reflect on their career journey. Clarify long-term career goals. Develop stronger professional branding. Improve career storytelling. Identify future opportunities. Understand career progression within international organizations. 3. Managerial Competencies Participants will examine important management skills including: Strategic thinking Problem-solving Team supervision Organizational effectiveness Staff development Professional accountability Performance management 4. Peacebuilding and Development Perspectives The program strengthens participants understanding of: International development Peacebuilding Humanitarian coordination Sustainable development International cooperation UN systems Global governance 5. Hiroshima Learning Experience One of the most unique features of the course is learning directly from Hiroshima. Participants will: Visit important historical sites. Learn about post-war recovery. Study peacebuilding from a historical perspective. Understand resilience and transformation. Reflect on leadership during times of crisis. Networking Opportunities Beyond classroom learning, the program places significant emphasis on professional networking. Participants will have opportunities to: Meet professionals from numerous countries. Connect with experts from Hiroshima University. Network with UNITAR facilitators. Engage with Ministry of Foreign Affairs officials. Exchange experiences with fellow peacebuilding professionals. Join a long-term international alumni network. Course Facilitators Training sessions will be facilitated by highly experienced professionals specializing in: Leadership development Career coaching International development Peacebuilding Human resources Organizational leadership Participants will benefit from practical insights drawn from years of experience working within international organizations and development institutions. Course Schedule The program consists of: Online Orientation Early December 2026 Introduction to participants Explanation of pre-course assignments In-Person Training January 7 – January 13, 2027 Locations include: Hiroshima University Mirai Crea UNITAR Hiroshima Daiwa Roynet Hotel Hiroshima Yoyogi Olympic Center, Tokyo Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) United Nations University (UNU) Participants will also travel from Hiroshima to Tokyo as part of the official program. Language of Instruction The entire training will be conducted in English . Applicants are therefore expected to possess strong spoken and written English communication skills. Who Can Apply? The program welcomes professionals from diverse professional backgrounds and nationalities. Applicants may come from: International organizations Government ministries Public institutions NGOs NPOs Civil society organizations Private companies Humanitarian organizations Eligible Professional Fields Professionals working in any of the following areas are encouraged to apply: Administration Communications Economics Engineering Finance Human Resources Humanitarian Assistance Information Technology Law Political Affairs Procurement Public Health Security Supply Chain Management Environmental Management International Development Peacebuilding Related professional disciplines Experience Requirements Applicants should possess at least seven years of relevant professional experience . The program particularly welcomes professionals at: P3 level P4 level P5 level Equivalent experience from outside the UN system is also accepted. Additional Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Have at least seven years of relevant professional experience. Be fluent in English. Demonstrate strong interest in peacebuilding and development careers. Show commitment to working with international organizations. Be available to attend the entire course. Although previous experience within the United Nations is not mandatory , familiarity with UN systems and international development frameworks is considered an advantage. Number of Participants Approximately 20 participants will be selected. The cohort will include: Japanese professionals International participants The small class size ensures interactive discussions and personalized learning. Financial Support For International Participants The program offers substantial financial support. Covered International participants will receive: Free participation Free accommodation throughout the training period Transportation between designated Japanese airports and Hiroshima Transportation from Hiroshima to Tokyo Not Covered Participants must cover: International airfare to Japan Return airfare from Japan Meals Personal expenses Any transportation beyond the officially covered routes For Japanese Participants Japanese participants receive: Free participation However, they are responsible for: Accommodation Meals Domestic transportation Estimated personal expenses are approximately 120,000 Japanese Yen . Certificate of Completion Upon successfully completing the program, participants will receive an Official Certificate of Completion from Hiroshima University. Graduates will also become members of the Global Peace and Development Career Network (GPAD) , giving them continued access to: Alumni networking Professional collaborations Future learning opportunities International exchanges Participant Responsibilities Selected participants are expected to: Attend every training session. Participate actively throughout the program. Submit daily evaluations. Complete the final evaluation. Respond to future alumni and career follow-up surveys from Japan s Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Selection Process Applications will be reviewed based on the submitted application materials. Applicants whose first language is not English may be invited to participate in an online interview to assess English language proficiency. Successful applicants will be notified by early November 2026 . How to Apply Interested applicants should follow these steps carefully: Download the Mid-Career Course FY2026 Application Form HERE Complete the application form in Microsoft Word format . UN staff may replace the Employment Information section with their UN Personal History Form while clearly indicating their staff category, level, and contract type. Type your name where the signature is requested. Save the completed application as a Word document (PDF submissions are not accepted). Email the completed application to: gpad-midcareer@office.hiroshima-u.ac.jp Use the following email subject: 2026 Mid-Career Course application (Your First Name Last Name – Country) Application Deadline Deadline: Monday, August 31, 2026, at 5:00 PM (Japan Time). Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so applicants are encouraged to submit their documents well before the deadline. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Mid-Career Course FY2026 offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including: International leadership training. Career development coaching. Expert mentorship. Exposure to UN frameworks. Professional networking. Cross-cultural collaboration. Learning from Hiroshima s resilience. Certificate from Hiroshima University. Membership in the GPAD international alumni network. Enhanced competitiveness for leadership positions within international organizations. Visit HERE to learn more about the Mid-Career Course FY2026 in Japan Additional Information Applicants should note the following: Submitted application documents will not be returned. Personal information will only be used for selection, communication, travel, accommodation, and visa arrangements where necessary. Hiroshima University complies with Japan s Personal Information Protection Act. Official photographs may be taken during the program and used for public communication purposes. Contact Information For additional inquiries, applicants may contact: Global Peace and Development Career Network (GPAD) International Office, Hiroshima University Kagamiyama 1-3-2 Higashi-Hiroshima City Hiroshima Prefecture 739-8511 Japan Email: gpad-midcareer@office.hiroshima-u.ac.jp Website: https://gpad.hiroshima-u.ac.jp/mid-eng/ Conclusion The Mid-Career Course FY2026 at Hiroshima University represents a valuable opportunity for experienced professionals committed to advancing peacebuilding and international development. Supported by Japan s Ministry of Foreign Affairs and implemented in partnership with UNITAR, the program combines leadership training, career development, historical learning, and global networking into an intensive one-week experience. Participants will gain practical leadership skills, deepen their understanding of resilience and transformation, and join an international community of professionals working to promote peace and sustainable development worldwide. If you have the required experience and aspire to take your leadership and international career to the next level, this program offers an excellent platform to expand your knowledge, strengthen your professional profile, and build lasting global connections. For more global training short courses opportunities visit OFY HERE The post Mid-Career Course FY2026 in Japan: Fully Funded Leadership and Career Development Program for Peacebuilding and Development Professionals at Hiroshima University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Regional Dialogue Academy “Dialogue for Resilience” 2026 in Lithuania: Fully Funded International Learning Programme for Educators, Youth Workers, Journalists, Civic Leaders and Community Professionals
summary: Apply for the Regional Dialogue Academy “Dialogue for Resilience” 2026 in Vilnius, Lithuania Applications are now open for the Regional Dialogue Academy Dialogue for Resilience 2026 , an international learning programme designed to strengthen democratic resilience, civic participation, and dialogue across societies facing conflict, displacement, polarization, and disinformation. Organized by the Eastern European Network for Citizenship Education (EENCE) and the Democratic Initiatives Incubator , this unique academy will bring together approximately 30 professionals from the Eastern Partnership region and diaspora communities to participate in an immersive learning experience in Vilnius, Lithuania , from 22–26 September 2026 . The programme provides an exceptional opportunity for educators, civil society practitioners, journalists, artists, researchers, youth workers, and community leaders to exchange knowledge, develop innovative approaches to dialogue, and establish long-term regional collaborations aimed at strengthening democratic societies. Participation in the academy is completely free , with accommodation, meals, learning materials, international travel reimbursement, local transportation, and visa-related costs covered by the organizers within specified reimbursement limits. If you are passionate about civic education, peacebuilding, community engagement, or democratic participation, this academy offers an outstanding platform to expand your expertise while connecting with professionals from multiple countries. About the Regional Dialogue Academy 2026 Modern societies across Europe and neighboring regions continue to experience unprecedented social and political challenges. Armed conflicts, forced displacement, misinformation, declining public trust, political polarization, and social fragmentation have created an urgent need for effective dialogue and inclusive civic participation. Recognizing these challenges, the Regional Dialogue Academy Dialogue for Resilience has been created as an international educational initiative that empowers professionals working directly with communities. Rather than being a traditional conference or lecture-based programme, the academy is designed as an interactive learning environment where participants can: Share practical experiences. Exchange successful methodologies. Learn from internationally experienced facilitators. Collaborate across borders. Develop innovative community-based dialogue initiatives. Build sustainable professional networks. Participants will explore how dialogue can become one of the strongest tools for rebuilding trust, strengthening democratic values, and creating resilient communities capable of addressing today s complex challenges. Programme Dates The Regional Dialogue Academy will take place according to the following schedule: 22 September 2026 – Arrival of participants 23–25 September 2026 – Academy programme 26 September 2026 – Departure Programme Location The academy will be hosted in: Vilnius, Lithuania Vilnius provides an inspiring setting for intercultural learning, democratic dialogue, and regional cooperation, making it an ideal venue for this international programme. Objectives of the Academy The Regional Dialogue Academy aims to: Strengthen democratic resilience. Promote inclusive dialogue across divided societies. Build practical skills in civic education. Encourage regional cooperation. Foster cross-border partnerships. Support educators and community professionals. Develop practical initiatives that improve civic participation. Encourage collaborative solutions to current democratic challenges. Ultimately, the programme seeks to equip participants with practical tools they can immediately implement within their own communities. What Participants Will Learn Over three intensive training days, participants will engage in a wide range of interactive learning sessions covering today s most pressing civic and democratic issues. Facilitating Dialogue in Divided Communities Participants will explore techniques for: Managing difficult conversations. Building trust among conflicting groups. Encouraging respectful communication. Creating safe dialogue spaces. Addressing community tensions. Trauma-Sensitive Education and Community Work The programme will introduce practical approaches for working with communities affected by: Armed conflict. Forced migration. Social trauma. Displacement. Violence. Participants will learn how to facilitate inclusive learning while recognizing the psychological impacts experienced by different communities. Democratic Resilience and Civic Participation Sessions will focus on: Strengthening democratic institutions. Encouraging citizen participation. Supporting active citizenship. Promoting democratic values. Building resilient communities. Memory, Identity and Difficult Conversations Participants will examine how historical memory, identity, and cultural narratives influence public dialogue. Topics include: Collective memory. National identity. Historical conflicts. Reconciliation. Sensitive public discussions. Inclusion, Diversity and Social Cohesion Participants will explore methods for creating more inclusive societies by promoting: Equal participation. Diversity. Mutual understanding. Social cohesion. Respect for different perspectives. Countering Disinformation Through Civic Education With misinformation becoming an increasing challenge worldwide, the academy will examine: Media literacy. Critical thinking. Civic education strategies. Public awareness. Community resilience against disinformation. Designing Participatory Learning Experiences Participants will also gain practical experience in creating engaging educational activities through: Interactive learning. Collaborative workshops. Community-based projects. Participatory education methods. Civic engagement exercises. Interactive Learning Approach The academy emphasizes practical participation rather than passive learning. Participants will engage in: Interactive workshops. Facilitated dialogue sessions. Practical simulations. Collaborative design sessions. Group discussions. Peer learning. Case studies. Reflection exercises. Experience sharing. Team-based activities. This hands-on methodology enables participants to immediately adapt newly acquired skills within their own professional settings. Beyond the Academy: National Dialogue Labs One of the academy s most valuable features is that learning continues long after the event concludes. Following the programme, participants will become part of National Dialogue Labs , which are local initiatives designed to bring dialogue and civic education into: Schools. Universities. Community organizations. Cultural institutions. Civil society organizations. Public spaces. Youth initiatives. These initiatives allow participants to implement what they have learned while receiving continued methodological support from the project team. Long-Term Regional Network Participants will also join an expanding regional network of professionals committed to: Democratic resilience. Citizenship education. Civic participation. Community engagement. Peacebuilding. Dialogue facilitation. Cross-border cooperation. This network provides valuable opportunities for future collaboration beyond the academy itself. Who Should Apply? The organizers encourage applications from professionals already engaged in community work and committed to strengthening democratic societies through dialogue. Eligible applicants include: Educators. Teachers. Youth workers. Civil society practitioners. Artists. Cultural practitioners. Journalists. Media professionals. Researchers. Community leaders. Representatives of public institutions. Facilitators. Professionals working in civic education. Professionals involved in democratic participation. Previous involvement with the EENCE network is welcomed but is not required . APPLY HERE Eligible Countries Applications are open to professionals currently based in: Armenia Azerbaijan Georgia Moldova Ukraine Germany Poland Lithuania The programme also welcomes professionals from Eastern Partnership countries who currently reside elsewhere in Europe but continue supporting citizenship education initiatives within their home countries or communities. Working Languages The academy will be conducted in: English Russian Participants should be able to actively participate in at least one of these languages. Interpretation between English and Russian will be available during selected programme sessions. Financial Support Participation is completely free. Selected participants will receive: Accommodation in twin rooms. Meals throughout the programme. Learning materials. International travel reimbursement. Local transportation within Vilnius. Visa costs (where applicable, within reimbursement limits). Insurance costs (within reimbursement limits). Detailed travel arrangements will be communicated to selected participants after acceptance. Participant Selection Process Approximately 30 participants will be selected through an open and competitive application process. Applications will be evaluated based on: Professional experience. Motivation. Ability to apply the academy s methodologies. Potential impact within local communities. Ideas for future dialogue initiatives. The organizers will also strive to ensure diversity by selecting participants from various: Countries. Professional backgrounds. Perspectives. Experiences. Equal participation will also be considered during the selection process. Why You Should Apply The Regional Dialogue Academy offers significantly more than a short-term training programme. Participants will benefit from: International professional networking. Practical dialogue facilitation skills. Civic education methodologies. Trauma-sensitive educational approaches. Collaborative project development. Cross-cultural exchange. Long-term professional partnerships. Access to experienced international facilitators. Continued engagement through National Dialogue Labs. Membership in a growing regional democratic resilience network. Professionals committed to building stronger communities and promoting democratic participation will gain valuable knowledge, practical experience, and lasting international connections. Visit HERE to learn more about the Regional Dialogue Academy “Dialogue for Resilience” 2026 in Lithuania Application Deadline The application deadline is 30 July 2026 . Applications submitted after this date may not be considered. Interested applicants are encouraged to complete and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid missing this opportunity. For questions regarding the programme, applicants may contact: DialogueAcademyEENCE@gmail.com Conclusion The Regional Dialogue Academy Dialogue for Resilience 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for professionals working in education, media, civil society, culture, research, and community leadership to strengthen their capacity to foster dialogue in increasingly complex societies. By combining practical learning, international collaboration, civic education, and long-term community engagement, the academy empowers participants with the tools necessary to address polarization, promote democratic participation, and strengthen resilience within their communities. Beyond the intensive programme in Vilnius, participants will continue their journey through National Dialogue Labs and a growing regional network dedicated to advancing citizenship education and meaningful dialogue across Eastern Europe and beyond. If you are committed to creating more inclusive, resilient, and democratic societies, this fully funded academy offers an exceptional opportunity to learn, collaborate, and make a lasting impact. For more global conference/training opportunities visit OFY HERE The post Regional Dialogue Academy “Dialogue for Resilience” 2026 in Lithuania: Fully Funded International Learning Programme for Educators, Youth Workers, Journalists, Civic Leaders and Community Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Refugees International Careers 2026: Apply for Humanitarian and Advocacy Jobs Supporting Refugees Worldwide
summary: Looking to build a meaningful career in humanitarian work? Refugees International is currently recruiting talented professionals for several full-time positions across advocacy, communications, finance, and program management. If you are passionate about protecting the rights of refugees, internally displaced people, asylum seekers, and vulnerable communities around the world, this is an excellent opportunity to join one of the world s leading independent humanitarian advocacy organizations. Refugees International works to improve the lives of people who have been forced to flee conflict, persecution, climate disasters, and humanitarian crises. Through research, policy advocacy, partnerships, and public engagement, the organization influences governments and international institutions to create lasting solutions for displaced populations. This article provides a comprehensive overview of the available positions, the organization, who should apply, and how to submit your application. About Refugees International Refugees International is an independent humanitarian organization dedicated to advocating for lifesaving assistance, human rights, and long-term solutions for refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced persons (IDPs), and stateless people around the globe. Unlike organizations that provide direct humanitarian aid, Refugees International focuses on influencing policy and decision-makers by conducting field research, producing evidence-based reports, and engaging governments and international organizations. The organization strives to ensure that people forced to flee receive protection, dignity, and opportunities to rebuild their lives. Its mission centers on creating a more welcoming and compassionate world for those seeking refuge. Why Consider a Career at Refugees International? Working at Refugees International offers professionals the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian efforts while collaborating with experts in refugee protection, international development, human rights, and policy advocacy. Employees have the chance to: Influence humanitarian policies. Advocate for displaced populations. Conduct impactful research. Work alongside global experts. Support vulnerable communities. Contribute to international human rights initiatives. Develop expertise in humanitarian affairs. Join a mission-driven nonprofit organization. Help shape global refugee protection policies. These careers are ideal for individuals who want their professional work to create measurable social impact. Current Full-Time Job Openings Refugees International is currently accepting applications for the following positions. 1. Senior Advocate for Displaced Women and Girls’ Rights This senior-level advocacy role focuses on protecting and promoting the rights of displaced women and girls affected by conflict, displacement, and humanitarian emergencies. Responsibilities may include: Leading advocacy campaigns. Conducting policy analysis. Producing reports and recommendations. Engaging governments and international organizations. Representing Refugees International in global forums. Supporting gender-responsive humanitarian policies. This role is particularly suitable for professionals with expertise in gender equality, women s rights, refugee protection, and international humanitarian law. Learn More 2. Refugee Leadership and Partnerships Program Coordinator This position supports refugee-led organizations and develops partnerships that strengthen refugee leadership across humanitarian responses. Key responsibilities may include: Coordinating partnership activities. Supporting refugee leadership initiatives. Managing stakeholder relationships. Assisting with program implementation. Facilitating communication among partners. Monitoring project progress. Applicants with experience in program coordination, nonprofit management, or international development may find this role especially relevant. Learn More 3. Middle East Program Officer The Middle East Program Officer contributes to research and advocacy focused on displacement and humanitarian issues affecting the Middle East region. Potential duties include: Monitoring regional developments. Conducting research. Drafting policy papers. Supporting advocacy campaigns. Coordinating with regional partners. Preparing reports and briefings. This opportunity is well suited for professionals with regional expertise, policy research experience, or humanitarian backgrounds. Learn More 4. Media and Communications Lead The Media and Communications Lead helps increase awareness of humanitarian crises by developing strategic communication initiatives. Responsibilities may include: Managing media relations. Creating communication strategies. Producing digital content. Supporting public campaigns. Working with journalists. Promoting organizational research and advocacy. Professionals with communications, journalism, digital media, public relations, or advocacy experience are encouraged to explore this opportunity. Learn More 5. Accountant The Accountant supports the financial management of Refugees International s operations. Typical responsibilities may include: Financial reporting. Budget management. Grant financial administration. Maintaining accounting records. Ensuring financial compliance. Supporting organizational audits. Candidates with accounting qualifications and nonprofit financial management experience may be particularly competitive. Learn More Areas of Impact Working at Refugees International allows professionals to contribute to issues such as: Refugee protection Human rights advocacy Women s rights Gender equality Humanitarian response International development Forced displacement Statelessness Climate displacement Migration policy Emergency response Global humanitarian governance Who Should Apply? These opportunities are ideal for professionals who are passionate about humanitarian work and international advocacy. Relevant backgrounds may include: International Relations Human Rights Law Public Policy Political Science International Development Gender Studies Communications Journalism Finance Accounting Program Management Nonprofit Management Regional Studies Humanitarian Affairs Benefits of Working at Refugees International Although individual benefits vary by position, working with Refugees International offers numerous professional advantages, including: Opportunity to work with an internationally recognized nonprofit organization. Meaningful work supporting refugees and displaced communities. Career development within the humanitarian sector. International policy and advocacy experience. Collaboration with global experts. Participation in impactful research. Exposure to international humanitarian issues. Opportunities to influence public policy. Mission-driven workplace. Applicants should review each vacancy individually for position-specific benefits and employment terms. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Visit the official Refugees International Careers page. Review the available job openings. Select the position that best matches your qualifications and experience. Carefully read the full job description. Prepare the required application materials. Submit your application through the official recruitment process. APPLY NOW Application Deadline The Refugees International Careers webpage does not specify a universal application deadline . Each vacancy may have its own recruitment timeline, so applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before positions are filled. Why These Opportunities Matter Global displacement continues to affect millions of people worldwide due to conflict, persecution, political instability, and climate-related disasters. Organizations like Refugees International play a critical role in advocating for policies that protect displaced populations and improve humanitarian responses. Joining Refugees International offers professionals the opportunity to transform their expertise into meaningful action while helping shape international refugee policy and advancing the rights of vulnerable communities. Whether your strengths lie in advocacy, communications, finance, research, or program coordination, these career opportunities provide a chance to contribute to global humanitarian efforts. Conclusion Refugees International s current recruitment drive presents an excellent opportunity for experienced professionals seeking careers that combine purpose with impact. With openings spanning advocacy, communications, finance, and regional programming, successful candidates will join a respected organization committed to protecting refugees and displaced populations worldwide. If you are passionate about humanitarian action, international development, and human rights, consider exploring these vacancies and submitting your application through the official Refugees International Careers portal. Visit the OFY website for more Job Opening Opportunities. The post Refugees International Careers 2026: Apply for Humanitarian and Advocacy Jobs Supporting Refugees Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GIFTS Fellowship 2026–2027: Apply for the Global Institute Fellowship for Transforming Education Systems
summary: About the Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future The Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future works with individuals and organisations worldwide to generate and share knowledge about transforming classrooms, schools and education systems. Its long-term vision is that every child should receive the education, support and opportunity required to shape a better future for themselves and their communities. The organisation contributes to this vision by developing education leaders while generating evidence, insights, tools and resources that can accelerate education-system improvement. The Global Institute emerged as an initiative of Teach For All. It draws on the experience of Teach For All’s international network of locally led organisations, teachers, alumni leaders and education practitioners. Its approach is based on cross-border learning, practical evidence, participatory inquiry, human-centred leadership development and collaboration among practitioners, students, researchers and policymakers. Rather than treating education reform as a purely technical matter, the Global Institute focuses on the people, relationships, leadership capabilities and institutional conditions required to produce sustainable change. What Is the GIFTS Fellowship? GIFTS stands for the Global Institute Fellowship for Transforming Systems . It is a global programme created for education leaders seeking to transform education systems through collective leadership, innovation and practical system-level action. The programme recognises that leaders responsible for education reform face increasingly complicated challenges. Education systems must respond to technological change, economic disruption, climate-related pressures, inequality and evolving expectations about the knowledge and capabilities young people need. System leaders are expected to improve learning outcomes, strengthen institutional performance, mobilise teachers and school leaders, coordinate stakeholders and maintain political and public support for reform. Despite these responsibilities, many education leaders have limited opportunities to engage deeply with peers from other systems or to examine the practical leadership challenges involved in large-scale reform. The GIFTS Fellowship was developed to address this gap. It will connect education-system leaders from different countries so they can learn from one another, examine real reform challenges, access experienced mentors and develop stronger locally rooted but globally informed visions for education. Purpose of the GIFTS Fellowship The central purpose of the programme is to help education-system leaders develop the vision, relationships, leadership capabilities and networks required to achieve transformation at scale. The fellowship is based on the understanding that education systems should do more than deliver academic content. They should help students develop purpose, agency, leadership and the ability to contribute to a better future. During the fellowship, participants will investigate major questions concerning education-system transformation, including: What outcomes should modern education systems seek to achieve for every student? How can the practices, structures and institutional culture of an education system be aligned behind a shared educational purpose? What strategies can improve the quality of learning taking place in classrooms? What forms of leadership are required to sustain education transformation? How can system leaders mobilise teachers, school leaders, policymakers and other stakeholders around a common reform agenda? How can change be made equitable, scalable and sustainable instead of remaining limited to individual schools or short-term pilot projects? The programme therefore combines leadership development with practical inquiry. Fellows will not only discuss education reform theoretically; they will examine concrete challenges from their own professional environments and test new approaches through action, reflection and peer feedback. Number of Participants and Programme Duration The GIFTS Fellowship will select 20 influential education-system insiders from diverse countries . The programme will run for approximately one year, beginning with a virtual launch event in October 2026 and concluding with a virtual graduation in September 2027. Most of the programme will be delivered virtually through: System-change laboratory sessions; Masterclasses; Leadership-development sessions; Peer-learning activities; Coaching; Mentoring; Roundtable discussions; and Shared inquiry. The fellowship also includes a compulsory four-day in-person system immersion in January 2027. Participation in the three-day Better Future Forum in May 2027 is optional. Who Should Apply? The programme is designed for influential and innovative leaders working at the education-system level. Potential applicants may include: Senior or rising officials working in ministries of education; Government education policymakers; Political advisers or officials responsible for education reform; Regional or provincial education-system leaders; Leaders of government education programmes; Professionals responsible for teacher development, curriculum reform or system performance; Civil-society leaders working through sustained partnerships with government; Leaders of organisations implementing education reform at national or regional scale; and Other education-system insiders with credible influence over policy, institutional practices or large-scale implementation. The programme is not primarily intended for individuals whose work is limited to a single classroom, school or short-term community project unless they also exercise broader system-level influence. Applicants should be able to demonstrate that their current position, professional credibility or institutional relationships allow them to influence significant change across a region or country. Qualities Expected from GIFTS Fellows The official selection profile emphasises leadership orientation and system-level influence rather than a specific academic degree or number of years of experience. Competitive applicants are expected to demonstrate the following qualities. 1. Long-Term Commitment to Children and Education Systems Applicants should show that improving the institutions and systems surrounding children is a sustained professional commitment rather than a temporary interest. Evidence may include leadership of education reforms, public policy work, institutional development, teacher-support initiatives or long-term government partnerships. 2. Commitment to Holistic Student Development Fellows should support an educational purpose extending beyond examination results or narrow academic achievement. The programme seeks leaders committed to helping students develop the knowledge, values, purpose, agency and capabilities required to shape their lives and contribute to society. This commitment should apply to all students, regardless of their background or economic circumstances. 3. Capacity to Build Collective Leadership Education-system transformation cannot normally be achieved by one organisation or individual. Applicants should therefore demonstrate an ability to mobilise and develop leadership among policymakers, administrators, teachers, school leaders, communities and partner organisations. 4. Ability to Navigate Complex Political Environments Government education reform often involves competing priorities, institutional resistance, resource constraints and political considerations. Applicants should be able to work constructively within these conditions while maintaining focus on long-term education outcomes. 5. Influence at National or Regional Scale The fellowship is intended for leaders capable of affecting significant system-level change. Applicants should explain the level at which they operate, the institutions or stakeholders they influence and the scale of the reform work they lead. 6. Global Leadership Potential Selected fellows should have the potential to contribute not only within their own countries but also to the broader international education ecosystem. They should be willing to share experience, learn across geographical boundaries and contribute insights that may support education leaders elsewhere. Main Components of the GIFTS Fellowship Cohort-Based Learning Model Selected participants will join a curated international cohort of education-system leaders. The cohort model is intended to create sustained and trusting relationships among fellows. Instead of attending isolated training sessions, participants will learn together over an extended period, examine one another’s reform experiences and provide feedback on real leadership challenges. This model can help participants move beyond superficial networking and develop a durable community of professional peers. System Change Lab Each fellow will explore a practical problem connected to their education-system work. Through the System Change Lab, participants will investigate the problem, test approaches and generate insights through action, reflection and feedback from other fellows. The laboratory model is particularly relevant to leaders dealing with problems that do not have straightforward technical solutions, such as: Improving learning quality across a large number of schools; Aligning different institutions behind a shared reform strategy; Strengthening teacher motivation and professional development; Scaling successful innovations; Building public and political support for reform; Reducing educational inequality; or Sustaining reforms through leadership or policy transitions. Required In-Person Immersion Event In January 2027, fellows will participate in a four-day in-person immersion within an education system undergoing transformation. The immersion will allow participants to engage with ministers, practitioners, schools and other members of the fellowship cohort. This component is compulsory. Applicants should therefore consider their professional availability and ability to travel before applying. Masterclasses and Roundtables The programme will include private sessions with experienced global education leaders. These sessions are expected to address not only successful reforms but also the trade-offs, setbacks, implementation failures and political challenges involved in education-system transformation. The closed-door format is intended to encourage candid discussion of issues that may not ordinarily be addressed in public conferences or formal policy presentations. Shared Inquiry Fellows will participate in structured collective inquiry around major education-system questions. Shared inquiry enables participants to compare experiences across different countries while recognising that education reforms must remain grounded in local history, culture, politics and institutional conditions. The objective is not to copy policies from one country to another. Instead, fellows will examine underlying principles and determine how global insights may be adapted to their own systems. Triad Model of Coaching and Mentoring The published programme describes a three-part support model consisting of: Policy mentors , who provide subject-matter and policy expertise; Leadership coaches , who help fellows navigate implementation, uncertainty and difficult trade-offs; and Peer coaches , who provide accountability, feedback and shared learning. This structure recognises that system leaders require technical advice, leadership support and credible peer relationships. Better Future Forum Fellows will have the opportunity to participate in the Global Institute’s annual Better Future Forum. The forum is a three-day in-person event scheduled for May 2027. Participation is optional. It offers participants an additional opportunity to connect with leaders and stakeholders from the wider global education ecosystem. Lifelong Community The programme is intended to create relationships extending beyond the formal fellowship period. Participants will be able to exchange practices across different regions, maintain contact with other system leaders and potentially visit fellow participants in their respective countries. The lifelong-community element is designed to build an expanding global network of leaders committed to education-system transformation. Benefits of Participating in the GIFTS Fellowship The official page presents the fellowship primarily as a leadership-development and professional-learning opportunity. It does not advertise a cash award or personal stipend. Selected fellows can expect to benefit from: Participation in an exclusive cohort of 20 education-system leaders; A year-long structured leadership-development journey; Virtual masterclasses with experienced education leaders; Access to policy mentoring and leadership coaching; Peer coaching, accountability and feedback; Participation in a practical System Change Lab; A required four-day international system immersion; Exposure to education reforms operating in other national contexts; Closed-door discussions about reform successes, failures and trade-offs; Stronger understanding of collective leadership; Development of a locally rooted and globally informed education vision; Opportunities to strengthen national or regional reform strategies; Connections with global education policymakers and practitioners; Optional participation in the Better Future Forum; and Membership in a long-term global community of education-system leaders. Important Funding and Cost Information The official programme page does not currently specify whether fellows will receive: A stipend; An honorarium; Salary replacement; Visa sponsorship; International airfare; Accommodation; Meals; Local transport; or Full or partial financial support for the required immersion event. Applicants should not describe the programme as “fully funded” unless the Global Institute later publishes explicit confirmation. Because the four-day January 2027 immersion is compulsory, applicants should review the application form and forthcoming fellowship blueprint carefully for information about travel and participation costs. GIFTS Fellowship Timeline Stage Date Applications opened 1 June 2026 Application deadline 4 September 2026 Selection announcement 5 October 2026 Virtual launch event October 2026 Virtual lab, masterclasses, peer and leadership sessions November–December 2026 Required four-day in-person immersion January 2027 Virtual lab, masterclasses, peer and leadership sessions February–April 2027 Optional three-day Better Future Forum May 2027 Virtual lab, masterclasses, peer and leadership sessions June–August 2027 Virtual graduation September 2027 The official webpage presents October 2026 as both the selection and launch period and provides 5 October 2026 as the specific selection-announcement date. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the GIFTS Fellowship is: 4 September 2026 Applicants are advised to submit well before the deadline to avoid technical difficulties or incomplete responses. The official page refers applicants to a fellowship blueprint containing application guidance. At the time the page was reviewed, one section indicated that the blueprint would be available soon. Candidates should therefore continue checking the official page for the complete guidance document and any clarification concerning programme funding, travel or participation requirements. How to Apply for the GIFTS Fellowship Applicants must submit their information through the official online application form linked on the GIFTS Fellowship webpage. A strong application should clearly explain: The applicant’s current education-system role; The national or regional scale at which the applicant operates; The education challenge the applicant is trying to address; The applicant’s record of influencing policy, institutions or implementation; How the applicant has developed collective leadership; How the applicant has worked through political or institutional complexity; The applicant’s commitment to equitable and holistic student development; The practical problem the applicant may wish to examine during the System Change Lab; and How participation in the fellowship could strengthen the applicant’s capacity to achieve system-level transformation. Applicants should use concrete evidence rather than relying on general statements about leadership. Quantifiable examples, policy achievements, implementation responsibilities and evidence of institutional influence will make an application more credible. Nomination Process The Global Institute also accepts nominations of exceptional education-system leaders. A colleague, institution or professional contact may nominate a leader considered to be working at the forefront of education transformation. However, a nomination is not the same as an application. It does not guarantee admission or automatic consideration for the final cohort. When a nominee appears to meet the eligibility criteria, the Global Institute may invite that individual to submit a full application. Candidates who already know about the opportunity and meet the requirements should therefore submit the formal application rather than relying solely on nomination. Application Advice for Prospective Fellows Because the fellowship targets influential system leaders, applicants should present themselves as active drivers of education reform rather than passive participants seeking general professional development. A competitive application should: Identify a defined system-level challenge; Explain why the challenge matters for students; Describe the applicant’s authority, influence or access to decision-makers; Present evidence of reforms, programmes or policies previously led; Show how the applicant works with government and other stakeholders; Demonstrate an understanding of collective leadership; Discuss the political and institutional complexity of the applicant’s work; Explain how international peer learning could strengthen local reform; Show willingness to contribute experience to the cohort; and Confirm readiness for the required in-person immersion. Applicants should avoid presenting a purely academic proposal unless it is connected to practical education-system leadership and implementation. Conclusion The GIFTS Fellowship 2026–2027 offers a distinctive professional-development opportunity for education leaders capable of influencing reform at national or regional scale. Through a small international cohort, system-change laboratories, masterclasses, mentoring, coaching, peer inquiry and an in-person system immersion, participants will examine the practical leadership required to transform complex education systems. This programme is particularly relevant for government officials, education policymakers, political-office representatives and civil-society leaders who maintain substantive, long-term partnerships with government. Successful applicants will need to demonstrate more than an interest in education. They must show sustained commitment, system-level influence, political and institutional awareness, collective leadership capacity and the potential to contribute to the international education community. Applications close on 4 September 2026 . Prospective applicants should review the official programme page carefully, monitor it for the fellowship blueprint and confirm the funding arrangements for the required in-person components before submitting their applications. For more global opportunities, click HERE . The post GIFTS Fellowship 2026–2027: Apply for the Global Institute Fellowship for Transforming Education Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26): Help Shape Africa’s Digital Future
summary: The African Union Commission (AUC) has officially opened the Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival 2026 (AU InnoFest 26) , inviting innovators, youth organizations, technology experts, entrepreneurs, policymakers, academia, development partners, and ecosystem builders to contribute to one of Africa s premier innovation and digital transformation events. Scheduled to take place from 29–30 September 2026 at the African Union Commission Headquarters in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia , AU InnoFest 26 will convene innovators, policymakers, business leaders, researchers, and young entrepreneurs from across Africa to explore solutions that will shape the continent s digital future. Under the theme Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty, the festival aims to strengthen youth-led innovation ecosystems while advancing Africa s ambitions in Artificial Intelligence (AI), digital transformation, technological innovation, and digital sovereignty. If you have a compelling session idea that can inspire meaningful dialogue, foster collaboration, or promote innovative solutions, this is your opportunity to help shape the official conference agenda. About AU InnoFest 26 The African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest) is the African Union s flagship platform dedicated to youth innovation, entrepreneurship, emerging technologies, and digital transformation across Africa. The event serves as a meeting point for: Young innovators Technology entrepreneurs Policymakers Researchers Development organizations Government institutions Civil society Private sector leaders Innovation hubs Academia International partners Through interactive sessions, networking opportunities, policy discussions, exhibitions, and collaborative engagements, AU InnoFest seeks to accelerate Africa s digital transformation agenda while empowering young Africans to become leaders of technological innovation. Theme of AU InnoFest 26 The 2026 edition will be held under the theme: Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty. The theme reflects Africa s growing commitment to: Artificial Intelligence innovation Digital transformation Youth empowerment Home-grown technological solutions Digital inclusion Policy development Responsible AI Innovation ecosystems Skills development Cross-sector collaboration Festival Objective The primary objective of AU InnoFest 26 is to strengthen vibrant youth-led ecosystems that advance Africa s AI capabilities and digital sovereignty through: Innovation Policy engagement Strategic partnerships Capacity building Digital skills development Knowledge sharing Collaborative action The festival recognizes that Africa s digital future depends on empowering young innovators to create locally relevant technological solutions while influencing policies that promote sustainable digital growth across the continent. Why Submit a Session Proposal? Selected session organizers will have the opportunity to: Contribute to the official AU InnoFest 26 programme. Share innovative ideas with a continental audience. Showcase successful projects and initiatives. Lead discussions on emerging digital trends. Connect with policymakers, investors, innovators, and development partners. Influence conversations on Africa s AI and digital transformation agenda. Expand professional networks across Africa. Increase visibility for their organization and initiatives.
