Who Should Submit a Proposal? The African Union welcomes session proposals from a broad range of stakeholders involved in innovation and digital development. Potential applicants include: Youth organizations Innovation hubs Startup founders Universities Research institutions Technology companies Civil society organizations Government agencies Development partners Entrepreneurs Artificial Intelligence experts Digital transformation specialists Policy professionals Community leaders Regional organizations What Makes a Strong Session Proposal? Successful proposals should align with the overall objectives of AU InnoFest 26 by encouraging meaningful discussions around: Artificial Intelligence Digital sovereignty Innovation ecosystems Youth entrepreneurship Digital inclusion Technology policy Skills development Emerging technologies Digital governance Cross-sector partnerships Startup ecosystems Future workforce development Proposals should demonstrate clear relevance to the conference theme while offering practical insights and opportunities for audience engagement. Important Information Before You Apply Applicants should carefully prepare all required information before beginning the submission process. You must have the following details ready: Full names of all proposed speakers Professional titles or designations Email addresses of speakers Moderator information Panelist details Having this information available before starting the application will help ensure a smooth submission process. Data Protection and Privacy The African Union Commission places significant importance on protecting applicants personal information. Information collected through the Call for Session Proposals will be used solely for: Reviewing submissions Evaluating proposals Managing the selection process Administering AU InnoFest 26 Personal information collected may include: Names Contact information Professional affiliations Speaker biographies Access to this information will be restricted to: Authorized members of the AU InnoFest 26 organizing team Approved programme review partners involved in the evaluation process If a proposal is selected, certain information may be published in: Official conference programmes Promotional materials Event communications Speaker profiles Conference schedules The African Union Commission confirms that personal information will not be shared with third parties for commercial purposes. Applicant Responsibilities By submitting a proposal, applicants confirm that: They have obtained consent from every proposed speaker. All submitted information is accurate. They agree to the African Union Commission processing the submitted information for the purposes of AU InnoFest 26. Applicants are encouraged to verify all information before submission to avoid delays during the review process. Why AU InnoFest Matters Africa is entering a transformative period where Artificial Intelligence, digital innovation, and technological sovereignty are becoming central to economic development. AU InnoFest provides an important platform for: Encouraging youth leadership. Promoting African innovation. Strengthening digital ecosystems. Influencing policy development. Supporting entrepreneurship. Building partnerships across sectors. Showcasing African technological solutions. Advancing digital inclusion. The festival serves as a catalyst for collaboration between governments, innovators, researchers, investors, and young entrepreneurs working toward a digitally empowered Africa. How to Apply Interested individuals and organizations should submit their session proposals through the official AU InnoFest 26 application form HERE Before submitting your proposal, ensure that you: Develop a session idea that aligns with the theme Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty. Identify and confirm your panelists, speakers, and moderators. Obtain the consent of all proposed speakers before sharing their personal information. Prepare the required information, including: Names of speakers Professional titles/designations Email addresses Session description Complete the official application form with accurate and complete information. Review your submission carefully before final submission. DEADLINE: 11th August, 2026 Event Details Event: African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest 26) Dates: 29–30 September 2026 Venue: African Union Commission Headquarters Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Theme: Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty Conclusion The African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest 26) offers innovators, entrepreneurs, policymakers, researchers, youth organizations, and digital ecosystem builders a unique opportunity to shape Africa s conversations around Artificial Intelligence, digital transformation, and technological sovereignty. By submitting a session proposal, applicants can contribute directly to one of the continent s most influential innovation platforms while sharing knowledge, fostering collaboration, and inspiring solutions that advance Africa s digital future. If your organization has expertise, innovative ideas, or successful initiatives that can help strengthen youth-led AI and digital ecosystems, now is the time to prepare a compelling session proposal and become part of AU InnoFest 26. For more GLOBAL CONFERENCE click HERE The post Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest 26): Help Shape Africa’s Digital Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 for Fresh Graduates in Ghana
summary: Are you a recent graduate preparing to undertake your mandatory National Service in Ghana? The Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 presents an exciting opportunity for talented young graduates to gain practical work experience in one of Ghana s leading telecommunications companies. Designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and professional employment, the Telecel Ghana National Service Program offers graduates the opportunity to work on real business projects, develop highly sought-after professional skills, collaborate with experienced industry professionals, and contribute to innovative solutions that impact millions of customers across Ghana. If you are passionate about technology, business, innovation, customer experience, and personal growth, this National Service opportunity could be the perfect start to your career. About the Telecel Ghana National Service Program Telecel Ghana is committed to connecting people and businesses through reliable and innovative telecommunications services. As one of Ghana s leading telecommunications providers, the company continues to invest in young talent by providing structured learning and career development opportunities. The National Service Program is specifically designed for graduates who are undertaking their mandatory National Service during the 2026–2027 service year . Participants will work within different departments across the organization, where they will gain hands-on experience while contributing to meaningful business initiatives. The program combines workplace learning, professional development, teamwork, and exposure to real-world business operations in a dynamic corporate environment. Why You Should Apply The Telecel Ghana National Service Program offers more than just a National Service placement. Successful applicants will have the opportunity to: Gain practical industry experience. Work with one of Ghana s leading telecommunications companies. Develop technical and professional workplace skills. Collaborate with experienced professionals across multiple departments. Build valuable professional networks. Improve communication and leadership abilities. Participate in impactful business projects. Enhance their employability after National Service. Experience a collaborative and innovative work environment. Roles and Responsibilities National Service personnel selected for the program will support the day-to-day operations of their assigned departments while contributing to organizational goals. Responsibilities include: 1. Support Daily Departmental Operations Participants will assist with the routine activities and operational functions of their assigned departments to ensure efficient service delivery. 2. Assist with Business Projects National Service personnel will support ongoing projects by: Conducting research. Collecting and analyzing data. Preparing reports. Coordinating administrative activities. Supporting project implementation. 3. Collaborate Across Teams Successful candidates will work closely with colleagues from different departments to ensure smooth execution of organizational initiatives. This collaborative environment allows participants to gain exposure to various aspects of the telecommunications industry. 4. Contribute Innovative Ideas Telecel Ghana encourages fresh graduates to contribute innovative thinking by: Identifying opportunities for improvement. Suggesting practical solutions. Supporting innovation within their teams. Bringing new perspectives to workplace challenges. 5. Demonstrate Telecel Ghana s Core Values Throughout the National Service period, participants are expected to uphold the organization s core values: Integrity Care Agility Collaboration Innovation These values form the foundation of Telecel Ghana s culture and guide everyday interactions within the workplace. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy the following requirements before applying. Educational Qualification Applicants should possess either: A Bachelor s degree; or A Higher National Diploma (HND) in any of the following fields: Business Administration Human Resources Marketing Finance Accounting Engineering Computer Science Information Technology (IT) Communications Related disciplines National Service Eligibility Applicants must be officially eligible to undertake the 2026–2027 National Service in Ghana. Professional Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Excellent written communication skills. Strong verbal communication skills. Microsoft Word proficiency. Microsoft Excel proficiency. Microsoft PowerPoint proficiency. Strong organizational abilities. Analytical thinking. Attention to detail. Professional work ethics. Personal Attributes Telecel Ghana is looking for graduates who possess: Leadership potential. Adaptability. Positive attitude. Strong willingness to learn. Integrity. Teamwork skills. Ability to work independently. Commitment to professionalism. Skills That Will Strengthen Your Application Although not mandatory beyond the stated requirements, applicants with the following competencies may be more competitive: Data analysis Report writing Research skills Problem-solving Customer service Time management Project coordination Digital literacy Communication skills Microsoft Office proficiency Working at Telecel Ghana Telecel Ghana provides an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning. National Service personnel will have opportunities to: Learn from experienced professionals. Understand corporate business operations. Develop workplace confidence. Build transferable career skills. Gain exposure to Ghana s telecommunications industry. Participate in meaningful projects that create business value. The company seeks to nurture young professionals who can contribute positively while preparing for long-term career success. Commitment to Diversity and Equal Opportunity Telecel Ghana is an equal opportunity employer. The company welcomes applications from qualified individuals without discrimination based on: Race Ethnicity Colour Gender Age Religion Disability Or any other legally protected status. This commitment ensures an inclusive workplace where talent, performance, and potential are valued above all else. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their application HERE Before applying, ensure that you: Confirm your eligibility for the 2026–2027 National Service . Prepare an updated CV highlighting your academic achievements, leadership experience, technical skills, internships, and extracurricular activities. Gather any supporting documents that may be required during the recruitment process. Complete the online application form accurately. Submit your application through the official recruitment portal before applications close. DEADLINE : 30th July, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of consideration. For more information about this opportunity click HERE Why This Opportunity Matters For many graduates, National Service serves as the first step into professional employment. Completing your National Service with Telecel Ghana provides valuable exposure to one of Ghana s most innovative industries while helping you develop technical expertise, leadership skills, workplace professionalism, and business experience. Whether your career interests lie in business, technology, engineering, finance, communications, marketing, or human resources, this program offers an excellent platform to launch your professional journey. Conclusion The Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 is an outstanding opportunity for fresh graduates seeking meaningful work experience in Ghana s telecommunications sector. Through practical assignments, cross-functional collaboration, and mentorship from experienced professionals, participants will develop valuable technical and professional competencies that extend far beyond the National Service year. If you are eligible for the 2026–2027 National Service and are eager to build your career in a dynamic, innovative, and inclusive work environment, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. Prepare a strong application, showcase your academic achievements and leadership experiences, and apply early to begin your professional journey with Telecel Ghana. title: Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarships in Germany 2026: Fully Explained Guide for International Master’s and PhD Students
summary: The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarship Programme is one of Germany s most respected funding opportunities for academically outstanding and socially engaged students. Through its Scholarship Department, the Rosa-Luxemburg-Stiftung provides financial assistance and comprehensive academic support to undergraduate, Master s, and doctoral students from Germany and around the world. Unlike many scholarship programmes that focus solely on academic excellence, the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation places equal emphasis on social responsibility, political engagement, and commitment to creating a more just society. The foundation actively supports students who demonstrate leadership, social awareness, and dedication to democratic values. The scholarship also seeks to reduce inequalities in higher education by giving preference to applicants from traditionally underrepresented backgrounds, including women, students from non-academic families, migrants, socially disadvantaged applicants, and people living with disabilities. If you are planning to pursue a Master s degree or PhD in Germany and have a proven record of academic excellence combined with social commitment, this scholarship may provide not only financial assistance but also access to an extensive academic and professional development network. About the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation The Rosa-Luxemburg-Stiftung (Rosa Luxemburg Foundation) is one of Germany s leading political foundations dedicated to education, research, democracy, and social justice. Its Scholarship Department supports highly qualified students who demonstrate: Outstanding academic performance Strong professional potential Active social engagement Political commitment aligned with the values of the foundation Motivation to contribute positively to society Beyond financial support, the foundation invests heavily in developing future leaders through networking, educational programmes, workshops, and mentoring. Scholarship Overview The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation offers several scholarship programmes, including: International Master s Scholarships International PhD Scholarships Study Scholarships for German students Lux like Studium Programme Domestic PhD Scholarships International applicants are primarily eligible for the Master s and PhD scholarship programmes. International Study Scholarships The International Study Scholarship is intended for foreign students who are temporarily living in Germany for higher education. Applicants are expected to return to their home countries after completing their studies. The programme supports students who have already been admitted to a German university and who intend to complete a Master s degree. Who Can Apply? International applicants must: Be temporarily residing in Germany Be enrolled at a state or state-recognized German university Be studying for a Master s degree Intend to return to their home country after graduation Meet all academic and language requirements Students enrolled in diploma or state examination programmes may also qualify if they have completed their foundational studies. Citizens of other European Union countries are also eligible to apply. Learn more PhD Scholarships The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation also offers scholarships to doctoral candidates from Germany and abroad. These scholarships support research across nearly every academic discipline. Applicants must already be accepted into a German university before applying. Funding is generally awarded for a period of three years. Eligible PhD Fields Funding is available for almost every discipline except: Medicine Dentistry Veterinary Medicine Students from scientific, technical, engineering, and research disciplines are particularly encouraged to submit applications. Learn more Scholarship Selection Criteria Applicants are evaluated using several important criteria. 1. Academic Excellence Applicants should demonstrate: Outstanding school results Excellent university performance Strong academic achievements High professional potential Evidence of above-average academic performance is required. 2. Political and Social Engagement One of the distinguishing features of the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarship is its strong emphasis on social commitment. Applicants should demonstrate involvement in activities such as: Community service Social justice initiatives Student organizations Civic participation Political engagement Volunteer work Human rights advocacy Environmental initiatives The foundation seeks applicants whose values align with its commitment to equality, democracy, and social justice. 3. German Language Requirement Applicants must possess German language proficiency of at least: Level B2 Proof of language ability is required during the application process. 4. Personal and Family Background The foundation considers an applicant s personal circumstances. Preference is often given to: Women Students from non-academic families Migrant backgrounds Economically disadvantaged students Applicants with disabilities Applicants who have overcome significant barriers while maintaining excellent academic performance Information on eligibility requirements and application forms for the scholarship can be found on the applications page. Fields of Study The scholarship supports students from all academic disciplines . The foundation actively encourages applications from: Science Technology Engineering Mathematics Social Sciences Humanities Arts Natural Sciences Technical disciplines Graduates from technical colleges are also encouraged to apply. Scholarship Benefits Although funding amounts follow German scholarship regulations and may vary depending on the programme, recipients benefit from far more than financial support. Scholarship holders gain access to: Financial assistance for studies Academic mentoring Networking opportunities Political education programmes Summer schools International workshops Educational trips Conferences Symposia Research support Academic writing workshops Methodology seminars Time management training Conflict management training Public speaking (rhetoric) training Leadership development Career development opportunities Strong alumni and academic networks More than 250 academic mentors and foundation staff members support scholarship recipients throughout their studies. Additional Support for Scholars Recipients become members of a large academic community that provides: Academic Development Research guidance Academic advising Methodological training Writing workshops Publication support Professional Skills Training includes: Leadership Communication Public speaking Negotiation Conflict resolution Self-management Time management International Exposure Participants may also attend: International workshops Study visits Summer schools Political education events Educational travel programmes Application Process The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation operates through a direct application process . Applicants must submit their applications using the official online application system. Important notes: Application forms are available only in German. Documents cannot be requested or submitted by post. Applicants must carefully review the eligibility requirements before applying. The new application round begins August 20, 2025 . Please inform yourself in advance on our website about the funding requirements for a study scholarship or doctoral scholarship . Application Deadline The annual application deadline is: 1 October Successful applicants begin receiving funding for the following summer semester , which starts on 1 April of the next year. Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare all required documentation well before the deadline. Please note the Application Portal Opens: 20 August 2026 APPLY NOW Contact Information Applicants seeking further guidance may contact the Scholarship Department directly. Please visit the German website for more information . Telephone: +49 30 44310-223 Email: studienwerk@rosalux.de Telephone consultation hours: Tuesday: 10:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. Wednesday: 2:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m. (September only) Thursday: 2:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m. Additional application information and official application forms are available on the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation s German-language scholarship website. Why You Should Apply The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarship is much more than a source of financial assistance. It is a comprehensive academic and leadership programme designed to support students who combine academic excellence with a commitment to making society more equitable and democratic. For international Master s and PhD students planning to study in Germany, the scholarship provides funding, mentorship, professional development, networking opportunities, and lifelong connections with scholars and practitioners committed to positive social change. If you possess outstanding academic achievements, strong social engagement, and German language proficiency, this programme represents an excellent opportunity to pursue higher education while becoming part of one of Germany s most respected academic communities. Conclusion The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarship Programme offers an exceptional opportunity for talented and socially committed students to pursue advanced studies in Germany. By combining financial support with extensive academic mentoring, leadership training, and professional development opportunities, the foundation helps scholars build successful careers while contributing meaningfully to society. Prospective applicants should carefully review the eligibility requirements, prepare all required documents in advance, and submit their applications before the 1 October deadline. For students who aspire to excel academically while promoting social justice and democratic values, the Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarship can serve as a transformative step toward achieving both educational and professional goals. Visit the OFY website for more Scholarship Opportunities. The post Rosa Luxemburg Foundation Scholarships in Germany 2026: Fully Explained Guide for International Master s and PhD Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027: Nominate Outstanding Global Leaders for One of Yale University’s Prestigious Leadership Programmes
summary: The Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027 is inviting nominations for exceptional emerging leaders from around the world who are making a meaningful impact in their fields and have the potential to become influential global changemakers. Hosted by the International Leadership Center at the Yale Jackson School of Global Affairs , the World Fellows Program seeks individuals who are challenging conventional thinking, creating innovative solutions, and addressing some of the world s most pressing issues. Although the official application period for the 2027 cohort has not yet opened, Yale University is encouraging members of the public to nominate outstanding candidates who they believe would make excellent World Fellows. The nomination process helps identify dynamic leaders from diverse backgrounds and sectors while raising awareness of the programme among potential applicants. Importantly, nominations are not required to apply for the programme when applications open, nor do they provide any advantage during the selection process. Instead, they serve as a way to ensure that exceptional leaders worldwide become aware of this prestigious opportunity. About the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program The Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program is a flagship international leadership initiative administered by Yale University through its International Leadership Center. The programme is designed to bring together accomplished professionals from different countries, sectors, and disciplines who have already demonstrated significant impact and are poised to become even more influential leaders. Rather than focusing solely on academic achievements or professional titles, the programme values individuals who have demonstrated creativity, innovation, leadership, and a commitment to solving global challenges. Each year, Yale searches for leaders who are: Creating innovative solutions to complex problems. Challenging outdated ideas and approaches. Demonstrating measurable impact in their communities or professions. Growing rapidly in their careers. Committed to expanding their leadership capacity. Motivated to contribute to positive global change. The programme welcomes individuals from a broad range of professional sectors, recognizing that today s global challenges require multidisciplinary leadership. Purpose of the Nomination Process The nomination process is intended to help Yale identify promising leaders who may benefit from participating in the World Fellows Program. Rather than relying solely on individuals discovering the programme independently, Yale encourages colleagues, mentors, supervisors, friends, and professional networks to recommend leaders whose work deserves wider recognition. The nomination initiative aims to: Expand awareness of the World Fellows Program. Identify outstanding emerging leaders globally. Increase diversity among future applicants. Reach talented individuals from different professional backgrounds. Encourage exceptional leaders to apply once applications officially open. What Kind of Leaders Is Yale Looking For? According to Yale, ideal World Fellow candidates are individuals who have already begun making a significant difference and continue to demonstrate exceptional promise. Successful nominees are expected to be people who: Think creatively and challenge conventional approaches. Develop innovative solutions to important problems. Demonstrate measurable impact in their work. Show strong leadership potential. Continue to grow professionally. Aspire to create lasting positive change. The programme seeks leaders who are not only accomplished but also eager to maximize their future impact. Sectors Represented by Potential World Fellows Yale s search for World Fellows spans numerous professional disciplines. Potential nominees may come from areas such as: Government Public policy International development Civil society Business Entrepreneurship Technology Journalism Media Education Research Public health Law Arts and culture Environmental sustainability Social innovation Human rights Community development International organizations The diversity of sectors reflects Yale s belief that solving global problems requires collaboration across different fields of expertise. Who Can Submit a Nomination? One of the programme s most inclusive features is that anyone can submit a nomination . Yale specifically notes that: Affiliation with Yale University is not required . Nominations are welcomed from people worldwide. Individuals may nominate more than one candidate. Anyone who knows a suitable leader is encouraged to submit a nomination. This open approach helps identify talented leaders from every region of the world. Important Nomination Guidelines Prospective nominators should keep the following guidelines in mind before submitting a nomination. 1. Self-Nominations Are Not Accepted Individuals should not nominate themselves . Anyone interested in becoming a World Fellow should instead sign up to receive notification when the official application becomes available. 2. Nomination Is Not Required Candidates do not need to be nominated to apply for the World Fellows Program. The nomination process is simply an outreach effort designed to help Yale identify exceptional leaders. 3. Nominations Do Not Influence Selection Yale clearly states that: Nominated candidates receive no preference during the application review process. Nomination does not increase an applicant s likelihood of selection. Every application is evaluated equally once applications officially open. 4. Multiple Nominations Do Not Improve Chances Receiving multiple nominations does not increase a candidate s chances of being selected. The programme evaluates candidates based on their own merits rather than the number of recommendations received. 5. Multiple Candidates May Be Nominated Individuals are welcome to nominate more than one outstanding leader. Each nomination should be submitted separately. Information Required About the Nominee The nomination form requests several pieces of information regarding the individual being nominated. Required information includes: First name Last name or surname Email address Nominators must also answer the following questions: Why would this person make a great Yale World Fellow? This section allows nominators to explain: Leadership achievements Professional accomplishments Innovation Community impact Vision Potential for future leadership Relationship with the Nominee Nominators are also asked to describe: How long they have known the nominee. The capacity in which they know the nominee. This provides important context for the recommendation. Information Required About the Nominator Individuals submitting nominations will also provide their own information. Required details include: First name Last name or surname Email address An optional field allows nominators to include: Professional title Organization What Happens After a Nomination Is Submitted? After a nomination is successfully submitted, Yale University sends an email directly to the nominated individual. The email informs the nominee that someone has encouraged them to apply to the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program. The message also includes: Additional information about the programme. Guidance regarding the future application process. This ensures that nominated individuals are aware of the opportunity when applications officially open. Current Application Status At present, the World Fellows Program is not accepting applications . Individuals interested in becoming World Fellows are encouraged to sign up for notifications so they can be informed when the official application process begins. Meanwhile, members of the public may continue submitting nominations for suitable candidates. Why Nominate Someone? Nominating an outstanding leader helps connect talented professionals with one of Yale University s premier international leadership programmes. By submitting a nomination, you can help ensure that deserving changemakers receive information about an opportunity that may significantly contribute to their leadership development and global impact. The programme aims to cultivate a community of leaders committed to addressing today s most pressing international challenges through innovation, collaboration, and thoughtful leadership. How to Submit a Nomination Individuals interested in nominating a candidate should: Identify an outstanding emerging leader. Confirm that the individual is not submitting a self-nomination. Complete the official nomination form. Provide accurate information about the nominee. Explain why the nominee would make an excellent Yale World Fellow. Describe your relationship with the nominee. Submit the completed nomination form. Following submission, Yale University will notify the nominated individual and provide additional information about the programme. Click HERE to learn more and to nominate an outstanding Global Leader for the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027. Conclusion The Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027 continues its commitment to identifying exceptional emerging leaders who are creating innovative solutions to global challenges. Through its open nomination process, Yale University invites people from around the world to recommend individuals whose leadership, creativity, and impact deserve international recognition. Although applications for the 2027 cohort are not yet open, the nomination process offers an excellent opportunity to help outstanding professionals learn about one of Yale University s most respected leadership programmes. Whether working in public service, business, civil society, technology, education, research, or another field, rising leaders with demonstrated impact and strong potential are encouraged to be nominated so they can consider applying when the official application cycle begins. Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027: Nominate Outstanding Global Leaders for One of Yale University s Prestigious Leadership Programmes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
